Short Description: Chirpy social birds found in every garden and city. Britain's most familiar garden companion.
Large plump pigeon with distinctive cooing call. Famous for raiding gardens and feeding on grain.
Male is glossy black with bright orange-yellow beak. Famous for melodious evening song.
Spotted breast and warm brown back. Repeats song phrases three times from high perches.
Britain's national bird with iconic red breast. Confiding garden visitor,  symbol of Christmas.
Tiny ball of blue and yellow plumage. Acrobatic feeder on garden plants and feeders.
Tiny diving duck of urban park lakes. Shy bird that disappears underwater when alarmed.
Elegant waterbird with striking crest and black ear tufts. Famous for elaborate courtship displays.
Boldly patterned black and white with long tail. Intelligent,  chattering and inquisitive bird.
Sleek blue-grey raptor that hunts garden birds with lightning speed and agility.
Colorful finch with rusty face and blue-grey head. Bouncing flight and cheerful song.
Striking colourful woodland bird with crest and loud raucous calls. Eats acorns and buries them.
Large spotted thrush that feeds on mistletoe berries and rowan berries in winter.
Stocky finch with black cap and brilliant rose-red breast in males. Visits fruit trees.
Black and white with dramatic red undertail. Distinctive loud drumming on dead wood.
Tiny brown bird with loud explosive song. Hunts insects in dense cover and shrubbery.
Brilliant turquoise and orange jewel of rivers and streams. Hunts small fish with precision dives.
Small dark bird that walks underwater hunting for aquatic invertebrates in mountain streams.
Most common owl with dark reddish plumage and eerie hooting call. Hunts small mammals at night.
Powerful forest raptor that hunts large birds and rabbits with incredible agility through trees.
Bird of open grassland famous for soaring song flight high in the sky at dawn.
Golden-headed bunting with rich song. Declining farmland bird that nests in hedgerows.
Large spotted thrush that visits fields and hedgerows in winter to feed on berries.
Smaller cousin of Fieldfare with distinctive red underwing stripe. Winter visitor to hedgerows.
Distinctive black and white plover with long crest. Tumbling display flight over fields.
Glossy black with white spots. Forms spectacular murmurations of thousands at dusk.
Delicate white heron with black legs and yellow feet. Now common on British wetlands.
Elegant grey and yellow wagtail that hunts flies over farm streams and ditches.
Ghostly white heart-faced owl that hunts voles over farmland at dusk and dawn.
Common broad-winged raptor seen soaring over fields. Hunts small mammals and rabbits.
Large dark goose with black neck and white chinstrap. Now common on British lakes.
Iconic pure white swan with orange bill. Graceful on water but aggressive in defense.
Commonest duck with green-headed drake and brown female. Found on every pond and river.
Black waterbird with red and yellow shield on face. Walks at water's edge searching for food.
Streaky brown warbler that nests in reed beds over water. Beautiful flowing song.
Paler streaky warbler with bold eyebrow stripe. Loud song from low perches in rushes.
Tall elegant grey heron standing motionless waiting for fish. Patient and deadly hunter.
Dark waterbird with long neck and crooked bill. Dives deep for fish in lakes and rivers.
Rare elegant raptor that hunts over wetlands and reed beds. Female is brown male pale.
Sleek raptor that hunts low over moorland and grassland with buoyant flight.
Delicate finch with red face and breast in males. Sings twittering song from gorse bushes.
White and buff bunting that arrives from Arctic in winter. Males very pale almost white.
Tiny colorful chat with black head and orange breast. Perches on gorse and heather.
Beautiful male with grey back and orange-red breast. Migrants passing through coasts.
Striking pied wading bird with long orange bill. Loud piping calls on rocky shores.
Small pale plover with bold black and white head pattern. Nests on shingle beaches.
Brilliant white seabird with black wing tips and yellow head. Plunges spectacularly for fish.
Elegant tern with grey and white plumage and red bill. Makes longest migration of any bird.
Large white gull with grey back and yellow bill. Familiar sound of seaside resorts.
Massive black-backed gull that takes small birds and rabbits. Largest gull in Britain.
Emerald green parrot with ring marking. Now established in London parks escaped from captivity.
Large green woodpecker with loud laughing call. Eats ants from ground and tree bark.
Large wading bird with long decurved bill and haunting call. Declining symbol of wildness.
Secretive reed bed specialist famous for booming call at night. Once nearly extinct in Britain.
Adorable seabird with colourful bill and round face. Britain's most beloved seabird icon.
Beautiful finch with red face and striking black and gold wings. Visits teasel and thistle seedheads.
Exotic looking starling-sized bird with crest. Winter visitor when berry crops fail in northern forests.
Elegant blue-black swallow with red face. Summer visitor skimming insects from air and water.
Spectacular fish-eating raptor with white underparts and bold eye stripe. Makes dramatic plunge dives.
Impressive reddish-brown raptor with forked tail. Britain's greatest conservation success story.
Striking bird with black and white head and white cheeks. Loud clear song and acrobatic feeder.
Tiny fluffy ball with very long tail. Visits gardens in small chattering flocks in winter.
Shy brownish bird with blue-grey head. Hunts insects under hedges and shrubs.
White-rumped swallow that builds mud nests under eaves. Aerial insect hunter.
Descendant of Rock Doves now living in cities everywhere. Comes in wild blue or pied colors.
Screaming fast swallow that catches insects high in air. Never lands except at nest.
Small crow with pale nape and eye. Forms flocks and nests in colonies on cliffs and buildings.
All-black crow that hunts alone or in pairs. Less social than Rooks but very intelligent.
Acrobatic blue-grey bird that walks headfirst down trees. Wedges seeds in bark crevices.
Small tit with distinctive black head and two white neck patches. Hides seeds throughout woodland.
Small elegant finch with yellow and black markings. Acrobatic feeder on alder and birch seeds.
Unique finch with crossed bill for extracting conifer seeds. Nomadic following seed crops.
Tiny brown bird that spirals up tree trunks hunting insects in bark. Elusive and hard to spot.
Tiny olive-green warbler that migrates through Britain in spring and autumn. Fluty song.
Small warbler with name reflected in song. First returning migrant herald of spring.
Legendary singer hidden in dense cover. One of finest songsters but rarely seen.
Small grey warbler with black cap in males. Beautiful flute-like song in spring.
Smart black and white warbler that hunts insects in the air. Summer visitor to woodlands.
Colorful game bird with long tail. Male iridescent with red face markings.
Plump game bird of grassland and cereal crops. Declining due to modern farming practices.
Deep red-brown grouse that lives on upland heather moors. Famous for driving out of cover.
Elegant black and white wagtail with constant tail-bobbing. Hunts insects over water.
Black crow with bare white face patch and shaggy leg feathers. Nests in colonies called rookeries.
Golden-speckled plover of upland grassland. Forms huge winter flocks with other plovers.
Smallest falcon that hovers above fields hunting voles. Rufous back and long black tail.
Long-winged owl that hunts low over open ground in daylight. Yellow eyes and fierce expression.
Black water bird with distinctive white frontal shield on face. Aggressive territorial bird.
Smart diving duck with chestnut head and cream forehead in males. Grazes on aquatic plants.
Tiny dabbling duck with green head and cream breast in males. Commonest small duck.
Striking reed bed specialist with long tail and black moustache in males. Beautiful orange plumage.
Secretive slate-blue rail that hides in reeds. Loud grunting calls betray its presence.
Camouflaged wading bird with long bill that probes for worms in mud. Zig-zag escape flight.
Black and white duck with distinctive tuft at back of head. Male glossy black white-sided.
Large grey-brown goose ancestor of domestic geese. Loud honking calls and strong fliers.
Black and white auk with long thin bill. Nests in huge colonies on sea cliffs.
Dumpy auk with short thick bill with white line. Takes flying fish with impressive diving.
Massive golden eagle reintroduced to Scotland. Hunts fish from lochs with awesome power.
Tiny fierce falcon that hunts small birds at speed. Male slate-blue female brown.
Majestic mountain eagle with golden crown. Rarest and most powerful British raptor.
Glossy black crow with red legs and bill. Rare bird of rocky cliffs in West Wales.
